title: RepeatY cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: raising RepeatY reps should still move decoded-media structure toward a denser vertical tile pattern rather than increasing free-form detail
raising RepeatY reps should materially bias the rendered decoded-media mix toward the right channel

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should gain stronger vertical tiling as reps rise without seam corruption
the stronger setting should remain legible on the committed clip
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pick up the same right-biased feedback comb character as the osc case
the rendered mix should remain stable without runaway or mute state
manualChecks: Does RepeatY remain visually useful on decoded media?
Does the right-bias still feel intentional rather than accidental channel imbalance?
